specification.topic-statement: The mail storage system persists, retrieves, and manages agent-to-agent messages in the database, enforcing session-scoped isolation and recipient ownership on deletion.

scope.in-scope: Message insertion, inbox retrieval (unfiltered and sender-filtered), mark-as-read, deletion with ownership validation, and session isolation semantics.
scope.boundary: How agents decide to send messages, wait for replies, or react to incoming mail is handled by the inter-agent messaging and wait synchronization subsystems. This topic covers only the storage operations.
Notifying a waiting agent that new mail has arrived is handled by the PostgreSQL notification layer. This topic does not emit notifications.

field.name: timestamp
field.note: Unix timestamp supplied by the caller; not generated by the database

field.name: read
field.note: false on insertion; set to true by the mark-as-read operation

field.name: session identifier
field.note: session scope; all operations filter by this value

behavior.session-isolation:
All storage operations are scoped to a session identifier. Messages are only visible within the session in which they were inserted. A message sent in one session is never returned by inbox queries in a different session, even if the sender and recipient identifiers match.
The session identifier is passed in by the caller on every operation. There is no enforcement that the caller provides a consistent or meaningful session identifier. Providing a mismatched identifier silently yields empty results.
behavior.message-insertion:
A message is inserted into the mail table with the provided sender identifier, recipient identifier, body, timestamp, session identifier, and an initial read value of false.
The timestamp value is supplied by the caller and inserted as given. The database does not generate or validate the timestamp.
On successful insertion, the database-assigned identifier is written back to the caller's message structure.
behavior.inbox-retrieval:
Two retrieval variants are supported:
All messages addressed to the recipient within the current session are returned, regardless of sender. Messages are ordered so that unread messages appear before read messages, and within each group, newer messages appear before older ones.
Only messages from a specific sender addressed to the recipient within the current session are returned. The same ordering applies: unread before read, newest first within each group.
Both variants return an empty result set without error when no matching messages exist. The full result set is returned in a single operation; there is no pagination or limit.
Messages that have been marked as read remain in the inbox and continue to appear in retrieval results. Reading a message does not remove it. Only the explicit delete operation removes a message.
behavior.mark-as-read: The mark-as-read operation updates a single message's read flag to true, identified by its database identifier. The operation succeeds even if the message identifier does not exist; no rows being updated is not treated as an error.
behavior.deletion:
The delete operation removes a single message identified by its database identifier, subject to a recipient ownership check. The deletion only succeeds if the message exists and the provided recipient identifier matches the message's recipient field.
If no message matching both the identifier and recipient is found, an error is returned. This is the only operation that enforces recipient ownership.
Insertion and retrieval do not verify that the caller has any relationship to the sender or recipient identifiers. Any caller can insert a message with arbitrary sender and recipient values or retrieve any recipient's inbox within the session.
